connectorUiConfig.title: StratoSecure Security Platform
connectorUiConfig.descriptionMarkdown: The StratoSecure Security Platform connector ingests ASPM findings, API inventory, remediation, scan summary and exception data pushed from the StratoSecure platform into Microsoft Sentinel via the Logs Ingestion API.

dataTypes.name: StratoSecure_ApiInventory_CL
dataTypes.lastDataReceivedQuery:
StratoSecure_ApiInventory_CL
 | summarize Time = max(TimeGenerated)
 | where isnotempty(Time)

dataTypes.name: StratoSecure_Exception_CL
dataTypes.lastDataReceivedQuery:
StratoSecure_Exception_CL
 | summarize Time = max(TimeGenerated)
 | where isnotempty(Time)

dataTypes.name: StratoSecure_Findings_CL
dataTypes.lastDataReceivedQuery:
StratoSecure_Findings_CL
 | summarize Time = max(TimeGenerated)
 | where isnotempty(Time)

dataTypes.name: StratoSecure_Remediation_CL
dataTypes.lastDataReceivedQuery:
StratoSecure_Remediation_CL
 | summarize Time = max(TimeGenerated)
 | where isnotempty(Time)

dataTypes.name: StratoSecure_ScanSummary_CL
dataTypes.lastDataReceivedQuery:
StratoSecure_ScanSummary_CL
 | summarize Time = max(TimeGenerated)
 | where isnotempty(Time)

value:
StratoSecure_Findings_CL
 | summarize LastLogReceived = max(TimeGenerated)
 | project IsConnected = LastLogReceived > ago(7d)

resourceProvider.permissionsDisplayText: read and write permissions are required.

customs.name: Microsoft Entra
customs.description: Permission to create an app registration in Microsoft Entra ID. Typically requires Entra ID Application Developer role or higher.

customs.name: Microsoft Azure
customs.description: Permission to assign Monitoring Metrics Publisher role on data collection rule (DCR). Typically requires Azure RBAC Owner or User Access Administrator role.

instructionSteps.title: 1. Create ARM Resources and Provide the Required Permissions
instructionSteps.description: This connector enables your StratoSecure platform to push ASPM findings, API inventory, remediation, scan summary and exception data directly to Microsoft Sentinel via the Azure Monitor Ingestion API.

parameters.content:
#### Automated Configuration and Secure Data Ingestion with Entra Application 
Clicking on "Deploy" will trigger the creation of Log Analytics tables and a Data Collection Rule (DCR). 
It will then create an Entra application, link the DCR to it, and set the entered secret in the application. This setup enables data to be sent securely to the DCR using an Entra token.

instructionSteps.title: 2. Configure Your StratoSecure Platform
instructionSteps.description: Use the following parameters to configure your StratoSecure platform to push data to the workspace.

parameters.label: Entra App Registration Application ID
parameters.placeholder: Deploy push connector to get the App Registration Application ID

parameters.label: Entra App Registration Secret
parameters.placeholder: Deploy push connector to get the App Registration Secret

parameters.label: Data Collection Endpoint Uri
parameters.placeholder: Deploy push connector to get the Data Collection Endpoint Uri

parameters.label: Data Collection Rule Immutable ID
parameters.placeholder: Deploy push connector to get the Data Collection Rule Immutable ID

properties.description: S1_SAST_Auth_BruteForce_AnalyticalRules Analytics Rule with template version 3.0.0

Detects applications with open SAST authentication findings that are
simultaneously experiencing brute force login attempts in Azure AD.
Correlates StratoSecure SAST findings with SigninLogs failure spikes.
properties.displayName: SAST Auth Finding Correlated with Brute Force
properties.query:
let lookback = 1h;
let bruteForceThreshold = 20;
let AuthFindings = StratoSecure_Findings_CL
    | where TimeGenerated >= ago(lookback)
    | where FixStatus =~ "open"
    | where Severity in ("Critical", "High")
    | where RuleId has_any ("auth", "jwt", "login", "session", "token", "password", "credential")
    | summarize FindingCount = count(), RuleIds = make_set(RuleId), Repos = make_set(Repository)
        by ApplicationId, StraTenantId;
let BruteForce = SigninLogs
    | where TimeGenerated >= ago(lookback)
    | where ResultType != "0"
    | summarize FailedAttempts = count(), DistinctUsers = dcount(UserPrincipalName),
        IPList = make_set(IPAddress, 20), FirstAttempt = min(TimeGenerated), LastAttempt = max(TimeGenerated)
        by AppId, AppDisplayName
    | where FailedAttempts >= bruteForceThreshold;
AuthFindings
    | join kind=inner BruteForce on $left.ApplicationId == $right.AppId
    | project TimeGenerated = LastAttempt, ApplicationId, AppDisplayName, StraTenantId,
        FindingCount, RuleIds, Repos, FailedAttempts, DistinctUsers, IPList

properties.displayName: SAST Auth Finding Correlated with Brute Force

properties.description:
Detects applications with open hardcoded-secret findings (CWE-798, CWE-259,
CWE-321, CWE-522) that have associated service principal sign-in anomalies.
Correlates StratoSecure secret findings with AAD SPN sign-in failures.
properties.displayName: Secret Exposure with Anomalous SPN Sign-In
properties.query:
let lookback = 1d;
let SecretFindings = StratoSecure_Findings_CL
    | where TimeGenerated >= ago(lookback)
    | where FixStatus =~ "open"
    | where CweId in ("CWE-798", "CWE-259", "CWE-321", "CWE-522")
    | where Severity in ("Critical", "High")
    | summarize FindingCount = count(), RuleIds = make_set(RuleId), Repos = make_set(Repository)
        by ApplicationId, StraTenantId;
let AnomalousSignins = AADServicePrincipalSignInLogs
    | where TimeGenerated >= ago(lookback)
    | where ResultType != "0" or ConditionalAccessStatus =~ "failure"
    | summarize FailedAttempts = count(), DistinctIPs = dcount(IPAddress),
        FirstAttempt = min(TimeGenerated), LastAttempt = max(TimeGenerated)
        by ServicePrincipalId, ServicePrincipalName
    | where FailedAttempts > 5 or DistinctIPs > 3;
SecretFindings
    | join kind=inner AnomalousSignins on $left.ApplicationId == $right.ServicePrincipalId
    | project TimeGenerated = LastAttempt, ApplicationId, ServicePrincipalName, StraTenantId,
        FindingCount, RuleIds, Repos, FailedAttempts, DistinctIPs

properties.displayName: Secret Exposure with Anomalous SPN Sign-In

properties.description: S3_BOLA_TrafficAnomaly_AnalyticalRules Analytics Rule with template version 3.0.0

Detects applications with open BOLA/IDOR findings (OWASP API1, API3, API5)
that also show anomalous 4xx error traffic in Azure API Management.
Correlates StratoSecure BOLA findings with APIM gateway error spikes.
properties.displayName: BOLA Finding with API Traffic Anomaly
properties.query:
let lookback = 1h;
let enumThreshold = 100;
let BolaFindings = StratoSecure_Findings_CL
    | where TimeGenerated >= ago(lookback)
    | where FixStatus =~ "open"
    | where OwaspCategory in ("API1", "API3", "API5")
    | where Severity in ("Critical", "High")
    | summarize FindingCount = count(), RuleIds = make_set(RuleId)
        by ApplicationId, StraTenantId;
let TrafficAnomalies = ApiManagementGatewayLogs
    | where TimeGenerated >= ago(lookback)
    | where ResponseCode in (403, 401, 404)
    | summarize ErrorCount = count(), DistinctCallers = dcount(CallerIpAddress),
        Paths = make_set(Url, 30), LastSeen = max(TimeGenerated)
        by ApiId, OperationId
    | where ErrorCount >= enumThreshold;
BolaFindings
    | join kind=inner TrafficAnomalies on $left.ApplicationId == $right.ApiId
    | project TimeGenerated = LastSeen, ApplicationId, StraTenantId,
        FindingCount, RuleIds, ErrorCount, DistinctCallers, Paths

properties.displayName: BOLA Finding with API Traffic Anomaly

properties.description:
Detects Critical and High severity findings older than 7 days that remain
open on internet-facing applications. Uses the InternetFacingApps watchlist
to scope results to externally exposed assets requiring urgent remediation.
properties.displayName: Critical Finding Overdue on Internet-Facing App
properties.query:
let maxAgeDays = 7;
let InternetFacingList = _GetWatchlist("InternetFacingApps")
    | project WL_ApplicationId = column_ifexists("ApplicationId", ""),
        WL_AppName = column_ifexists("AppName", ""),
        WL_Owner = column_ifexists("Owner", "");
StratoSecure_Findings_CL
    | where FixStatus =~ "open"
    | where Severity in ("Critical", "High")
    | extend AgeInDays = datetime_diff("day", now(), TimeGenerated)
    | where AgeInDays > maxAgeDays
    | join kind=inner InternetFacingList on $left.ApplicationId == $right.WL_ApplicationId
    | summarize TotalOverdueFindings = count(), OldestAge = max(AgeInDays),
        RuleIds = make_set(RuleId, 20), EndTime = max(TimeGenerated)
        by ApplicationId, WL_AppName, WL_Owner, StraTenantId
    | project TimeGenerated = EndTime, ApplicationId, AppName = WL_AppName, Owner = WL_Owner,
        StraTenantId, TotalOverdueFindings, OldestAge, RuleIds

properties.displayName: Critical Finding Overdue on Internet-Facing App

properties.description:
Detects internet-facing API endpoints with no authentication that handle
sensitive data classifications (PII, Financial, PHI, Confidential).
Uses StratoSecure_ApiInventory_CL from Phase 6 API inventory discovery.
properties.displayName: Unauthenticated API Endpoint with Sensitive Data
properties.query:
let sensitiveDataClasses = dynamic(["PII", "Financial", "PHI", "Confidential"]);
StratoSecure_ApiInventory_CL
    | where TimeGenerated >= ago(6h)
    | where InternetExposure == true
    | where AuthType =~ "none" or isempty(AuthType)
    | where DataClassification has_any (sensitiveDataClasses)
    | summarize EndpointCount = count(), Endpoints = make_set(Path, 50),
        Methods = make_set(Method), DataClasses = make_set(DataClassification),
        EndTime = max(TimeGenerated)
        by ApplicationId = ServiceName, StraTenantId
    | project TimeGenerated = EndTime, ApplicationId, StraTenantId,
        EndpointCount, Endpoints, Methods, DataClasses

properties.displayName: Unauthenticated API Endpoint with Sensitive Data

properties.description: S11_PCI_AuditWindow_AnalyticalRules Analytics Rule with template version 3.0.0

Detects PCI-scoped applications with open Critical/High findings when an
upcoming PCI-DSS audit is within 30 days. Correlates PCIApplications and
AuditCalendar watchlists to surface compliance risk before audit deadlines.
properties.displayName: PCI App with Open Findings Before Audit Window
properties.query:
let auditWindowDays = 30;
let PCIApps = _GetWatchlist("PCIApplications")
    | project WL_ApplicationId = column_ifexists("ApplicationId", ""),
        WL_AppName = column_ifexists("AppName", ""),
        WL_AuditDate = column_ifexists("AuditDate", ""),
        WL_Owner = column_ifexists("Owner", "");
let AuditCalendar = _GetWatchlist("AuditCalendar")
    | where column_ifexists("Framework", "") =~ "PCI-DSS"
    | extend UpcomingAudit = todatetime(column_ifexists("AuditDate", ""))
    | where UpcomingAudit between (now() .. now() + auditWindowDays * 1d)
    | project WL_Cal_ApplicationId = column_ifexists("ApplicationId", ""),
        Framework = column_ifexists("Framework", ""), UpcomingAudit;
StratoSecure_Findings_CL
    | where FixStatus =~ "open"
    | where Severity in ("Critical", "High")
    | join kind=inner PCIApps on $left.ApplicationId == $right.WL_ApplicationId
    | join kind=inner AuditCalendar on $left.ApplicationId == $right.WL_Cal_ApplicationId
    | summarize TotalOpenFindings = count(), MaxSeverityInt = max(SeverityInt),
        RuleIds = make_set(RuleId, 20), EndTime = max(TimeGenerated)
        by ApplicationId, WL_AppName, WL_Owner, Framework, UpcomingAudit, StraTenantId
    | extend MaxSeverity = case(
        MaxSeverityInt >= 9, "Critical",
        MaxSeverityInt >= 7, "High",
        MaxSeverityInt >= 4, "Medium",
        "Low")
    | project TimeGenerated = EndTime, ApplicationId, AppName = WL_AppName, Owner = WL_Owner,
        StraTenantId, Framework, UpcomingAudit, TotalOpenFindings, MaxSeverity, RuleIds

properties.displayName: PCI App with Open Findings Before Audit Window

metadata.description: Name of the Logic App playbook resource.
Name of the Microsoft Sentinel Log Analytics workspace.
Azure region for the Logic App. Defaults to the resource group location.
Base URL of the Jira instance, e.g. https://your-org.atlassian.net
Jira project key where tickets will be created.
Jira API token for authentication. Store in Key Vault and reference via Key Vault reference.
Email address of the Jira user associated with the API token.
Log Analytics workspace ID for audit log ingestion.
Log Analytics workspace primary or secondary key for the azureloganalyticsdatacollector connector.
When true, main actions execute after the approval condition evaluates. Set to false for fully automated execution without gating. Audit log is always written regardless of this setting.

metadata.title: StratoSecure - Create Jira Ticket
metadata.description: When a Microsoft Sentinel incident is created from a StratoSecure finding, this playbook opens a Jira bug ticket and writes an audit record to StratoSecure_PlaybookRuns_CL. Human approval is always required before the action executes.
metadata.prerequisites: StratoSecure Platform API key. Microsoft Sentinel workspace with Contributor access.
postDeployment: Grant the Logic App system-assigned managed identity the 'Microsoft Sentinel Responder' role on the Sentinel workspace.

metadata.description: Name of the Logic App playbook resource.
Name of the Microsoft Sentinel Log Analytics workspace.
Azure region for the Logic App. Defaults to the resource group location.
Azure DevOps organization name (the part after dev.azure.com/).
Azure DevOps project name where work items will be created.
Azure DevOps Personal Access Token with Work Items (Read & Write) scope. Store in Key Vault.
Log Analytics workspace ID for audit log ingestion.
Log Analytics workspace primary or secondary key for the azureloganalyticsdatacollector connector.
When true, main actions execute after the approval condition evaluates. Set to false for fully automated execution without gating. Audit log is always written regardless of this setting.

metadata.title: StratoSecure - Create Azure DevOps Work Item

postDeployment: Grant the Logic App system-assigned managed identity the 'Microsoft Sentinel Responder' role on the Sentinel workspace.

When true, main actions execute after the approval condition evaluates. Set to false for fully automated execution without gating. Audit log is always written regardless of this setting.

metadata.title: StratoSecure - Notify Microsoft Teams

metadata.prerequisites: StratoSecure Platform API key. Microsoft Sentinel workspace with Contributor access.
postDeployment: Grant the Logic App system-assigned managed identity the 'Microsoft Sentinel Responder' role on the Sentinel workspace.

classification.ClassificationReasonText: Resolved via StratoSecure: finding confirmed remediated or accepted as risk.

metadata.title: StratoSecure - Close Incident in Sentinel
metadata.description: When a StratoSecure finding is resolved or accepted, this playbook closes the corresponding Microsoft Sentinel incident as BenignPositive and writes an audit record to StratoSecure_PlaybookRuns_CL. Human approval is always required before the action executes.
metadata.prerequisites: StratoSecure Platform API key. Microsoft Sentinel workspace with Contributor access.
postDeployment: Grant the Logic App system-assigned managed identity the 'Microsoft Sentinel Responder' role on the Sentinel workspace.

metadata.description: Name of the Logic App playbook resource.
Name of the Microsoft Sentinel Log Analytics workspace.
Azure region for the Logic App. Defaults to the resource group location.
Azure Communication Services resource hostname (e.g. your-acs-resource.communication.azure.com). Do not include https:// prefix.
Set to true to authenticate with ACS via Logic App managed identity. Set to false to use ACS access key.
Azure Communication Services access key. Required only when AcsManagedIdentityEnabled is false. Store in Key Vault.
Verified sender email address in the ACS Email Communication Service.
Recipient email address for escalation notifications (e.g. soc-lead@your-org.com).
Log Analytics workspace ID for audit log ingestion.
Log Analytics workspace primary or secondary key for the azureloganalyticsdatacollector connector.
When true, main actions execute after the approval condition evaluates. Set to false for fully automated execution without gating. Audit log is always written regardless of this setting.

A security finding requires immediate attention.

Description@{triggerBody()?['object']?['properties']?['description']}

metadata.title: StratoSecure - Escalate to Owner via Email
metadata.description: When a Microsoft Sentinel incident from a StratoSecure finding remains unacknowledged, this playbook sends an escalation email via Azure Communication Services and writes an audit record to StratoSecure_PlaybookRuns_CL. Human approval is always required before the action executes.
metadata.prerequisites: StratoSecure Platform API key. Microsoft Sentinel workspace with Contributor access.
postDeployment: Grant the Logic App system-assigned managed identity the 'Microsoft Sentinel Responder' role on the Sentinel workspace.

properties.descriptionHtml:
Note: Please refer to the following before installing the solution:

• Review the solution Release Notes

• There may be known issues pertaining to this Solution, please refer to them before installing.

Hybrid ASPM solution correlating code-level SAST/SCA findings with Microsoft Defender for Cloud runtime evidence. Delivers regulatory compliance evidence (PCI-DSS 4.0, CNBV, SBP) and PR quality gates for banking security teams.

Data Connectors: 1, Analytic Rules: 6, Watchlists: 8, Playbooks: 7

Learn more about Microsoft Sentinel | Learn more about Solutions
